metadata.description: Consolidated plan to take a raw product idea from concept through launched, monitored software product. ~190 steps (down from 328 in v2) plus a 15-step feature template (down from 31). Designed for AI agent execution with human-in-the-loop via needs_clarification mechanism. Supports both greenfield and existing project onboarding via Phase -1. Every step has difficulty, tools_hint, and artifact_schema annotations.
metadata.clarification_mechanism: Any agent can trigger needs_clarification status. The assistant agent formulates the question, sends via Telegram, interprets the human reply, and resumes the task with clarification context. Reminders are sent every 24 hours if no response. After 72 hours, the orchestrator logs the blocker and pauses the pipeline.
metadata.artifact_convention: Each step reads input_artifacts from prior steps and produces output_artifacts. The orchestrator must pass artifact contents as context to the executing agent. Every artifact name must be globally unique across the entire plan.
metadata.template_convention: Steps with type=template are reusable sequences. They are instantiated by referencing the template_id and providing template_params. The implementation_backlog lists items that each instantiate the feature_implementation_template.

review_policy.escalation_message_template: Review cycle for step '{step_name}' has failed {max_review_cycles} times. Issues: {issues_list}. Human decision needed: fix manually, accept as-is, or provide direction.

revision_policy.description: Allows controlled feedback from later phases back to earlier phase artifacts without full phase re-entry.

allowed_revisions.mechanism: Architecture alignment check at sprint boundaries produces mini-ADRs and updates the relevant artifact sections in-place.

onboarding_policy.description: Controls behavior when the engine operates on an existing codebase rather than a greenfield project.

conditional_groups.description: If fewer than 3 direct competitors exist, run a lighter research path.

fallback_steps.name: competitor_feature_scan_lite
fallback_steps.instruction: Fewer than 3 direct competitors found. Do a lightweight feature scan of whatever direct competitors exist, plus analyze indirect competitors more deeply. For each competitor or indirect alternative: catalog visible features from their website, app store listing, and documentation. Build a combined feature matrix covering both direct and indirect competitors. Note: a thin competitive landscape is itself a signal â€” either blue ocean opportunity or no viable market. Flag this for the go/no-go assessment.
fallback_steps.done_when: competitor_features_raw and competitor_feature_matrix exist with whatever competitive data is available.

conditional_groups.description: Skip realtime design if no functional requirements need it.

fallback_steps.name: realtime_not_needed
fallback_steps.instruction: No realtime requirements identified. Document this decision and recommend the simplest future path to add realtime (e.g., SSE for notifications, WebSocket for collaboration). Output a brief document stating realtime is not needed for MVP with a migration path.
fallback_steps.done_when: realtime_design exists as a brief 'not needed' document with future migration path.

conditional_groups.description: Skip payment-related steps if the product is free.
Skip app store submission if no native mobile apps.
Skip file upload security testing if no upload functionality.
Skip SEO if product has no public web pages.
Skip email launch if no waitlist or email list exists.

phases.name: Existing Project Onboarding
phases.goal: Reverse-engineer an existing project into the plan's artifact format and determine execution starting point.
phases.condition: Only execute if input includes existing_codebase_path

phases.name: Idea Capture & Clarification
phases.goal: Turn a vague idea into a crisp, validated problem statement with clear constraints.

phases.name: Market & Competitive Research
phases.goal: Understand the market landscape, competitors, user needs, and regulatory environment.

phases.name: Product Strategy & Definition
phases.goal: Define what we are building, for whom, why it wins, and what the MVP scope is.

phases.name: Requirements Engineering
phases.goal: Convert product requirements into precise, testable technical requirements.

phases.name: Architecture & Technical Design
phases.goal: Design the complete technical foundation: stack, schema, APIs, infrastructure patterns.

phases.name: UX/UI Design Specification
phases.goal: Fully specify every screen, component, interaction, and responsive behavior.
phases.note: Can run in parallel with phase_4. Does not depend on tech stack decisions.

phases.name: Project Planning & Sprint Setup
phases.goal: Break all work into estimated, ordered, dependency-mapped sprints.

phases.name: Development Environment Setup
phases.goal: Set up repositories, tooling, CI/CD, infrastructure, and design system implementation.

phases.name: Core Implementation
phases.goal: Build every MVP feature through repeatable implementation cycles.

phases.name: Comprehensive Testing
phases.goal: Ensure reliability, performance, accessibility, and cross-platform correctness.

phases.name: Security Hardening
phases.goal: Audit and fix all security vulnerabilities before public exposure.

phases.name: Documentation
phases.goal: Document everything for users, developers, and operators.

phases.name: Legal & Compliance
phases.goal: Ensure legal coverage: ToS, privacy policy, GDPR, license compliance.

phases.name: Pre-Launch Preparation
phases.goal: Production infrastructure, monitoring, analytics, and final QA.

phases.name: Launch
phases.goal: Go live safely and announce to the world.

phases.name: Post-Launch Operations & Iteration
phases.goal: Monitor, stabilize, collect feedback, iterate, and grow.

steps.name: codebase_discovery
steps.instruction: Scan the repository and catalog everything. Use file_tree and index_workspace to get a complete picture. Produce codebase_inventory with sections: repository_info (git history length, branch structure, last commit date, contributor count), tech_stack_detected (languages with file counts, frameworks detected from config files, database type from connection strings or migration files, cache detected, hosting detected from CI/CD configs), project_structure (directory tree with purpose inference for each directory), dependencies (list all from package.json/requirements.txt/go.mod with versions), database_schema_current (from migration files or ORM schema: list all tables, columns, types, indexes), api_endpoints_current (from route files: list all endpoints with method, path, middleware), test_inventory (test file count, framework detected, coverage if measurable from config), ci_cd_detected (pipeline files found, stages defined, deployment targets), configuration_files (list all config files with purpose), documentation_found (README, docs folder, inline comments quality estimate).
steps.done_when: codebase_inventory exists with all sections populated from the existing codebase.

steps.name: code_quality_assessment
steps.instruction: Assess the codebase quality. Run the project's linter if configured, or apply standard linter for the detected language. Check: linting (errors and warnings count), type_safety (is TypeScript strict? are there any-types? is Python typed?), test_coverage (run coverage tool if possible, identify untested critical paths), code_patterns (consistent architecture? service layer separation? error handling pattern?), security_surface (hardcoded secrets scan, dependency vulnerability scan, auth implementation review), technical_debt_visible (TODO/FIXME/HACK comments, disabled tests, commented-out code, dead code). Produce: quality_score (1-10 per category), critical_issues (things that must be fixed before adding features), patterns_to_preserve (good patterns the codebase uses that we should follow).
steps.done_when: code_quality_report exists with per-category scores, critical issues, and patterns to preserve.

steps.name: artifact_synthesis
steps.instruction: For each artifact in the plan, attempt to synthesize it from the existing project. Produce two outputs. synthesized_artifacts: a collection where each entry has artifact_name, content (the synthesized artifact in the plan's expected format), source (where in the codebase this was extracted from), confidence (high: directly extracted from clear source, medium: inferred from multiple signals, low: guessed based on conventions, absent: could not determine). artifact_confidence_map: a flat mapping of artifact_name to confidence level. Artifacts to synthesize: tech_stack_decision (from package.json, configs), database_schema (from migrations/ORM), api_endpoints (from route files), auth_design (from auth middleware and token handling), frontend_architecture (from folder structure and component patterns), design_tokens (from CSS variables, Tailwind config, or theme files), business_rules (from validation logic in code), error_handling_strategy (from error middleware), logging_strategy (from logger config), caching_strategy (from cache usage in code). Do NOT synthesize: product strategy artifacts (vision, personas, USP) or market research artifacts.
steps.done_when: synthesized_artifacts and artifact_confidence_map exist covering all technical artifacts extractable from code.

steps.name: project_state_mapping
steps.instruction: Map the existing project's state onto the plan's step structure. For every step in the plan, determine its status: completed (all output artifacts exist with high confidence AND quality assessment shows no critical issues in that area), partially_completed (artifacts exist but with medium/low confidence, OR quality issues exist that need addressing), needs_execution (output artifacts are absent), not_applicable (the step doesn't apply), needs_human_input (the step produces strategic artifacts that cannot be extracted from code alone). Group results by phase. For each phase: calculate completion_percentage, list blocking_gaps (artifacts that are absent and block downstream phases), list quality_gaps (artifacts that exist but need improvement). Identify the effective_starting_phase: the earliest phase that has incomplete steps which block all downstream work.
steps.done_when: project_state_map exists with per-step status, per-phase completion percentage, and effective starting phase identified.

steps.name: onboarding_strategy
steps.instruction: Based on project_state_map, create the execution plan for this project. Determine execution_mode: greenfield_phases_only, implementation_continuation, hardening_and_launch, post_launch_operations, or selective_improvement. Present to human: project_state_map summary, detected execution_mode, proposed step sequence (which steps to run, which to skip), estimated effort, critical gaps that need human input. Trigger needs_clarification to get human confirmation.
steps.done_when: onboarding_plan exists with execution_mode, step sequence, and human confirmation received.

steps.name: convention_extraction
steps.instruction: Extract coding conventions that all future generated code must follow. Analyze: naming_conventions (camelCase vs snake_case for variables, PascalCase for components, file naming pattern), file_organization (where do new routes go? where do new components go? where do tests go relative to source?), import_style (absolute vs relative, alias usage, import ordering), error_handling_pattern, test_patterns (describe/it vs test, mocking approach, fixture patterns, assertion style), api_patterns (response format, pagination style, authentication attachment, validation approach), component_patterns (functional vs class, hooks usage, prop patterns, styling approach), state_management_patterns, git_conventions (branch naming from git log, commit message format from history). Output a conventions document that will be injected as context to every implementer and coder agent step.
steps.done_when: project_conventions exists with all detected patterns documented for agent consumption.

steps.name: human_strategic_input
steps.instruction: For artifacts that cannot be synthesized from code (product vision, target audience, business model, competitive positioning), collect this information from the human. Format questions based on what's missing. Present synthesized artifacts for human validation. Get human confirmation of the onboarding_plan execution mode and step sequence.
steps.done_when: strategic_context exists with human-provided product context filling gaps code couldn't provide.

steps.name: raw_idea_intake
steps.instruction:
The user's raw idea is provided in the Context Artifacts section below. Take it exactly as stated, preserving all details, aspirations, mentioned features, and constraints. Do not interpret, filter, or add anything. Structure the output as a document with sections: Original Statement, Mentioned Features, Mentioned Constraints, Mentioned Audience, Mentioned Goals. If any section has no content from the raw idea, mark it as 'Not specified'.

Output the complete document as your final answer in markdown format. Do NOT just write a file â€” your response text IS the artifact.
steps.done_when: A structured raw_idea_document exists that faithfully represents the original idea with zero interpretation added.

steps.name: problem_statement_extraction
steps.instruction: From the raw_idea_document, extract and articulate the core problem being solved. Output a structured problem statement with these fields: problem_description (1-3 sentences describing the pain), affected_audience (who has this problem), current_alternatives (how they cope today, if inferable), severity (how painful this is, if inferable), frequency (how often they encounter it, if inferable). Mark any field as 'Needs validation' if it cannot be confidently inferred from the raw idea.
steps.done_when: A structured problem_statement exists with all fields populated or explicitly marked as needing validation.

steps.name: assumption_identification
steps.instruction: Identify every assumption embedded in the raw idea. For each assumption, output: assumption_id (A-001, A-002...), statement (the assumption in clear language), category (one of: market, user, technical, business, legal, competitive), risk_if_wrong (what happens if this assumption is false), validation_method (how we could validate this). Common categories to check: 'Users exist who want this', 'Users will pay for this', 'This is technically feasible', 'No legal blockers exist', 'We can differentiate from competitors', 'The market is large enough'.
steps.done_when: A numbered assumption_list exists with at least 5 assumptions identified, each with all fields completed.

steps.name: scope_ambiguity_detection
steps.instruction: Review all input artifacts. Identify every ambiguity, undefined term, or scope question that must be resolved before proceeding. Generate a numbered list of open questions organized by category: Audience (who exactly?), Scope (what's in/out?), Business Model (how does this make money?), Constraints (budget, timeline, tech, team?), Platform (web, mobile, desktop?), Differentiation (why us over competitors?), Priority (what matters most: speed, quality, features, cost?). Each question should be specific enough that a yes/no or short answer resolves it.
steps.done_when: An open_questions_list exists with all identified ambiguities as clear, answerable questions.

steps.name: human_clarification_request
steps.instruction: Review the open_questions_list and assumption_list. Pick only the TOP 5 most impactful questions â€” the ones whose answers most change the project direction. Keep each question short (1-2 sentences max). For each, suggest 2-3 concrete answer options the human can pick from (e.g. 'A) Families B) Roommates C) Both'). Do NOT ask more than 5 questions â€” if in doubt, cut the less important ones. You MUST use the clarify action to send these questions to the human.
steps.done_when: clarification_request has been sent to the human via the clarify action.

steps.name: idea_brief_compilation_and_review
steps.instruction: Compile all inputs into a clean Idea Brief document with these sections: 1) Vision (one paragraph describing the aspiration), 2) Problem Statement (from problem_statement, updated with clarifications), 3) Target Audience (specific, from clarifications), 4) Proposed Solution (high-level, what the product does), 5) Key Constraints (budget, timeline, tech, from clarifications), 6) Initial Scope Boundaries (what's in for v1, what's explicitly out), 7) Assumptions (updated list, marking which were validated by clarifications), 8) Open Risks (remaining unknowns). Write in clear, professional prose. Then self-review: check for internal contradictions between sections, anything from clarification_answers not reflected, anything from raw idea lost or misrepresented, vague language that should be specific, and missing information. Fix any issues found. Output the final versioned document as idea_brief_final with a version number and date.
steps.done_when: idea_brief_final exists as a clean, self-reviewed, internally consistent document with all 8 sections.

steps.name: market_existence_research
steps.instruction:
Using the idea_brief_final, research whether a market exists for this problem. Search for: market size estimates (TAM, SAM, SOM if available), market growth rate, industry reports or analyst coverage, investment activity in this space (recent funding rounds), Google Trends data for related keywords, job postings in this space (indicates industry activity). Use web search tools. Check industry-specific sources. Output structured findings: market_size (with source), growth_trend (with source), investment_signals (list of recent fundings), search_interest_trend (direction), industry_reports (list with links), confidence_level (high/medium/low with justification).

IMPORTANT: After gathering data (1-2 searches max), produce your answer as a JSON object in your final response. Do NOT keep searching â€” use what you found. Your response text IS the artifact.
steps.done_when: market_existence_data exists with sourced data points covering market size, growth, and investment activity.

steps.name: target_audience_research
steps.instruction:
Research the target audience defined in idea_brief_final. Find: demographics (age, location, occupation, income level), psychographics (values, attitudes, lifestyle), online behavior (which platforms, subreddits, forums, communities they use), pain points (what they complain about related to this problem), current tools/solutions they use, willingness to pay signals (do they pay for similar tools?), technology adoption level. Search Reddit, Twitter/X, forums, Quora, Stack Overflow, Product Hunt discussions, industry surveys. Output structured findings per category with source links.

IMPORTANT: After gathering data (1-2 searches max), produce your answer as a JSON object in your final response. Do NOT keep searching â€” use what you found. Your response text IS the artifact.
steps.done_when: audience_research_data exists with demographic, psychographic, behavioral, and pain point data with sources.

steps.name: direct_competitor_identification
steps.instruction:
Identify all direct competitors: products solving the same problem for the same audience. For each competitor, collect: name, website_url, one_line_description, founding_year, funding_total (if known), team_size_estimate, platforms (web/ios/android/desktop), primary_market (geography), status (active/acquired/dead). Search: web search, Product Hunt, G2, Capterra, AlternativeTo, Crunchbase, App Store, Play Store. Find at least 5 competitors if they exist. If fewer than 3 exist, note this as a market signal (either blue ocean or no market).

IMPORTANT: After gathering data (1-2 searches max), produce your answer as a JSON object in your final response. Do NOT keep searching â€” use what you found. Your response text IS the artifact.
steps.done_when: direct_competitors_list exists as a structured list with all fields populated per competitor.

steps.name: indirect_competitor_identification
steps.instruction:
Identify indirect competitors: products that solve the same problem differently, or solve an adjacent problem for the same audience. For each, collect: name, website_url, how_they_relate, threat_level (high/medium/low). Also identify potential future competitors: large companies that could enter this space easily.

IMPORTANT: After gathering data (1-2 searches max), produce your answer as a JSON object in your final response. Do NOT keep searching â€” use what you found. Your response text IS the artifact.

OUTPUT SIZE: Keep each competitor entry under 200 words. Focus on name, URL, relation, and threat level.
steps.done_when: indirect_competitors_list exists with categorized indirect competitors and future threat assessment.

steps.name: competitor_feature_analysis
steps.instruction:
For each direct competitor in direct_competitors_list, catalog every feature visible from their: marketing website (features page, pricing page), documentation, free tier exploration, App Store/Play Store listing, Product Hunt page, demo videos. For each feature, record: feature_name, feature_description, which_competitor, tier_available (free/paid/enterprise), and category (core, secondary, integration, admin). Be exhaustive. Then build a feature comparison matrix. Rows: every unique feature (deduplicated and normalized across competitors). Columns: each competitor. Cell values: 'full', 'partial', 'none', 'unknown'. Group rows by feature category. Add a 'feature_frequency' column. Sort within each category by frequency descending.

IMPORTANT: After gathering data (1-2 searches max), produce your answer as a JSON object in your final response. Do NOT keep searching â€” use what you found. Your response text IS the artifact.

OUTPUT SIZE: Keep the feature matrix concise  -  max 10 features per competitor, one line each. Total output under 2000 words.
steps.done_when: competitor_features_raw and competitor_feature_matrix exist with comprehensive feature data and comparison table.

steps.name: competitor_pricing_analysis
steps.instruction:
For each direct competitor, document their pricing model completely: pricing_model_type, tiers (each with name, monthly_price, annual_price, key_limits), free_tier_scope, upgrade_triggers, enterprise_offering, pricing_page_url. Then analyze: market standard pricing range, most common model, pricing gaps, free-to-paid feature gates.

IMPORTANT: After gathering data (1-2 searches max), produce your answer as a JSON object in your final response. Do NOT keep searching â€” use what you found. Your response text IS the artifact.

OUTPUT SIZE: Keep pricing analysis concise  -  one paragraph per competitor. Total under 1000 words.
steps.done_when: competitor_pricing_analysis exists with per-competitor pricing details and cross-market pricing insights.

steps.name: competitor_review_and_sentiment
steps.instruction:
For each major competitor (top 5), collect user reviews and sentiment from: App Store, Play Store, G2, Capterra, TrustRadius, Product Hunt comments, Reddit mentions, Twitter/X mentions, Hacker News discussions. For each source: source_name, review_count_sampled, average_rating, positive_themes, negative_themes, notable_quotes (3-5 per competitor). Focus on recent reviews (last 12 months). Then synthesize sentiment. Per competitor: top_5_praised_aspects, top_5_pain_points, overall_sentiment, net_promoter_signal. Cross-competitor: universal_praises (table stakes), universal_complaints (opportunities), differentiation_signals.

IMPORTANT: After gathering data (1-2 searches max), produce your answer as a JSON object in your final response. Do NOT keep searching â€” use what you found. Your response text IS the artifact.

OUTPUT SIZE: Keep sentiment concise  -  max 5 bullet points per competitor for positives and negatives. Total under 1500 words.
steps.done_when: competitor_reviews_raw and competitor_sentiment_analysis exist with per-competitor and cross-competitor sentiment synthesis.

steps.name: competitor_ux_analysis
steps.instruction:
For each major competitor (top 5), evaluate their UX based on publicly available information: website structure, app store screenshots descriptions, user reviews mentioning UX, documentation quality. Do NOT try to analyze images directly â€” instead, assess UX from text descriptions, review comments about UI/UX, app store listing descriptions, and website copy. Evaluate each competitor for: design_quality (1-10), visual_consistency, information_density, navigation_clarity, onboarding_quality, accessibility_signals, mobile_quality, unique_ux_patterns, ux_weaknesses. Output per competitor with overall_ux_rank.

IMPORTANT: After gathering data (1-2 searches max), produce your answer as a JSON object in your final response. Do NOT keep searching â€” use what you found. Your response text IS the artifact.

OUTPUT SIZE: Keep UX evaluation concise  -  max 3 strengths and 3 weaknesses per competitor. Total under 1000 words.
steps.done_when: competitor_ux_evaluation exists with per-competitor UX assessment and comparative ranking.

steps.name: market_gap_analysis
steps.instruction:
Cross-reference features users request but no competitor provides, features where all competitors are 'partial', sentiment weaknesses common across competitors, pricing gaps. Also produce a SWOT analysis for each major competitor (top 3-5) with specific, evidence-based statements. Output a ranked list of market opportunities: opportunity_id, description, evidence, estimated_impact, difficulty_to_address, our_ability_to_exploit. Rank by impact x feasibility. Wire indirect_competitors_list into analysis to catch substitute threats.

IMPORTANT: After gathering data (1-2 searches max), produce your answer as a JSON object in your final response. Do NOT keep searching â€” use what you found. Your response text IS the artifact.
steps.done_when: market_gap_analysis exists with a ranked, evidence-based list of market opportunities and per-competitor SWOT.

steps.name: technology_trend_research
steps.instruction:
Research technology trends relevant to the product domain. Find: new APIs or services, AI/ML capabilities, emerging platforms, open-source tools that reduce build cost. For each: name, description, relevance_to_our_product, maturity_level (experimental/emerging/mature), adoption_risk.

IMPORTANT: After gathering data (1-2 searches max), produce your answer as a JSON object in your final response. Do NOT keep searching â€” use what you found. Your response text IS the artifact.
steps.done_when: technology_trends document exists with relevant tech trends and their applicability assessed.

steps.name: regulatory_research
steps.instruction:
Identify legal, regulatory, and compliance requirements for this product. Check: data privacy (GDPR, CCPA), industry-specific regulation (HIPAA, PCI-DSS, COPPA, FINRA), platform policies, accessibility law, intellectual property concerns, open-source license obligations. For each: requirement_name, applies_if, summary_of_obligations, compliance_effort, consequence_of_non_compliance.

IMPORTANT: After gathering data (1-2 searches max), produce your answer as a JSON object in your final response. Do NOT keep searching â€” use what you found. Your response text IS the artifact.
steps.done_when: regulatory_requirements exists with all applicable regulations identified and obligations summarized.

steps.name: market_research_synthesis
steps.instruction: Synthesize market and competitor research into a Research Summary under 500 words. Sections: Market Viability, Target Audience Profile, Competitive Landscape, Key Risks.
steps.done_when: research_summary exists as a well-structured summary document.

steps.name: market_research_report
steps.instruction: Using research_summary plus gap analysis, technology trends, regulatory data, and indirect competitors, compile the full Market Research Report. Sections: Executive Summary, Market Overview, Competitive Landscape, Market Gap Analysis, Recommendations.
steps.done_when: market_research_report exists as a comprehensive, well-structured document.

steps.name: research_quality_review
steps.instruction:
Review market_research_report for: 1) Claims without evidence, 2) Logical gaps, 3) Missing data, 4) Bias, 5) Internal contradictions, 6) Feature matrix and pricing completeness, 7) Gap analysis quality. Output: verdict (pass/fail/needs_minor_fixes), issues list.

IMPORTANT: After gathering data (1-2 searches max), produce your answer as a JSON object in your final response. Do NOT keep searching â€” use what you found. Your response text IS the artifact.
steps.done_when: research_review_result exists with clear quality assessment.

steps.name: go_no_go_assessment
steps.instruction:
Evaluate all research to produce a Go/No-Go recommendation. Score 1-10: market_attractiveness, competitive_feasibility, technical_feasibility, differentiation_potential, regulatory_risk. Compute weighted_score. Wire indirect_competitors_list into the feasibility assessment. If score > 6: Go. If 4-6: Conditional â€” trigger needs_clarification. If < 4: No-Go.

IMPORTANT: After gathering data (1-2 searches max), produce your answer as a JSON object in your final response. Do NOT keep searching â€” use what you found. Your response text IS the artifact.
steps.done_when: go_no_go_decision exists with scored assessment and clear recommendation.

steps.name: product_vision_and_positioning
steps.instruction: Craft a product vision statement: 'For [target audience] who [need], [product name] is a [category] that [key benefit]. Unlike [competitors], we [key differentiator].' Also write a tagline under 10 words. Then craft a product mission statement: 'We help [specific audience] [achieve specific outcome] by [how the product does it].' Under 30 words. Self-review for alignment with go_no_go_decision and market_research_report.
steps.done_when: product_vision and product_mission exist with vision statement, tagline, and mission.

steps.name: value_proposition_canvas
steps.instruction: Create a Value Proposition Canvas with Customer Profile (jobs, pains, gains) and Value Map (products_and_services, pain_relievers, gain_creators). Draw explicit connections between each pain_reliever and customer_pain, each gain_creator and customer_gain. Identify top 3 pain-gain fits.
steps.done_when: value_proposition_canvas exists with both sides populated and explicit pain-gain connections identified.

steps.name: unique_selling_proposition
steps.instruction: Define 1-3 USPs that are: Valuable, Unique, Credible, Communicable. Reference specific competitor weaknesses and market gaps. If multiple positioning strategies are viable, present all with trade-offs and trigger needs_clarification.
steps.done_when: unique_selling_proposition exists with 1-3 validated USPs.

steps.name: user_personas_and_journeys
steps.instruction:
Create 2-4 distinct user personas with: name, age, occupation, location, tech_savviness, goals, frustrations, current_tools, behavioral_patterns, quote, primary_pain. One persona should be the 'primary' persona. For each persona write 2-3 usage scenarios covering first-time use, regular use, and edge case/advanced use. Then map end-to-end user journey for primary persona through: awareness, consideration, signup, onboarding, first_value, engagement, mastery, expansion, advocacy. For each stage: user_actions, touchpoints, emotions, drop_off_reasons, metrics. Identify the 'aha moment'.

OUTPUT SIZE: Keep each persona under 300 words. Max 3 personas. Total under 1500 words.
steps.done_when: user_personas and user_journey_map exist with detailed personas, scenarios, and journey stages.

steps.name: feature_brainstorm
steps.instruction: Generate an exhaustive list of every possible feature from: competitor matrix (table stakes), value proposition canvas (pain relievers, gain creators), user scenarios (product actions), technology trends (tech-enabled features), USP implications. No filtering. For each: feature_id (F-001...), feature_name, description, source, category. Expect 50-200 features.
steps.done_when: feature_brainstorm_list exists with an exhaustive, categorized feature list.

steps.name: feature_prioritization
steps.instruction: Categorize every feature using MoSCoW: Must-have, Should-have, Could-have, Won't-have. Justify each. Must-haves should include: core auth, minimum features to deliver primary value proposition, regulatory requirements. Aim for 15-25 Must-haves, 10-20 Should-haves.
steps.done_when: feature_prioritization exists with every feature categorized and justified.

steps.name: mvp_scope_definition
steps.instruction: Define the MVP: smallest feature set that delivers core value proposition and validates primary USP. Include all Must-haves. Selectively include Should-haves for coherent UX. Output: mvp_feature_list, excluded_features_rationale, estimated_scope_size, mvp_value_statement. If scope >30 or <8 features, trigger needs_clarification.
steps.done_when: mvp_scope exists with a clear feature list, rationale, and scope size estimate.

steps.name: user_stories
steps.instruction:
For every feature in mvp_scope, write formal use cases and user stories. Each use case: use_case_id, feature_id, title, primary_actor, preconditions, trigger, main_flow, alternate_flows, exception_flows, postconditions, business_rules. Then convert each use case into user stories: story_id, use_case_id, epic, title, story ('As a [persona], I want [action], so that [benefit]'), acceptance_criteria (Given/When/Then), priority, estimated_complexity. Every acceptance criterion must be testable.

OUTPUT SIZE: Max 15 user stories. Each story under 100 words including acceptance criteria.
steps.done_when: user_stories exists with testable user stories covering all MVP features, each with Given/When/Then acceptance criteria.

steps.name: success_metrics_definition
steps.instruction: Define KPIs using AARRR: Acquisition, Activation, Retention, Revenue, Referral. For each: name, formula, data_source, target_value, measurement_frequency. Define north_star_metric with justification.
steps.done_when: success_metrics exists with AARRR metrics, targets, measurement methods, and a north star metric identified.

steps.name: monetization_strategy
steps.instruction: Define business model: pricing_model, tiers, free_tier_strategy, upgrade_triggers, billing_implementation, revenue_projections. If multiple viable models exist, present with trade-offs and trigger needs_clarification.
steps.done_when: monetization_strategy exists with pricing tiers, free/paid split, and revenue projections.

steps.name: prd_strategy_section
steps.instruction: Compile the strategy sections of the Product Requirements Document: Vision & Mission, Value Proposition, Unique Selling Proposition, Monetization Strategy. Write as a cohesive document, not just a list of artifacts.
steps.done_when: prd_strategy_section exists as a cohesive strategy document.

steps.name: prd_final
steps.instruction: Merge prd_strategy_section with user personas, MVP scope, user stories, journey map, and success metrics into the final Product Requirements Document. Self-review for contradictions, vague requirements, and missing sections. Add executive summary.
steps.done_when: prd_final exists as a comprehensive, self-reviewed, versioned PRD with all sections.

steps.name: functional_requirements_extraction
steps.instruction:
Extract every functional requirement from prd_final. For each: req_id (FR-001...), title, description, source_story_ids, priority, category (auth, data, ui, api, integration, notification, search, admin, billing). Every user story must map to at least one FR and vice versa.

OUTPUT SIZE: Max 20 functional requirements. Each under 100 words.
steps.done_when: functional_requirements exists as a numbered list where every requirement traces to user stories.

steps.name: nfr_performance_and_scalability
steps.instruction: Define performance NFRs: api_response_time (p50, p95, p99), page_load_time, time_to_interactive, LCP, FID, CLS, throughput, database_query_time. Adjust targets based on product type and audience. Then define scalability NFRs: concurrent_users, data_volume, file_storage, api_request_volume, background_job_volume, notification_volume. Include current, 6-month, and 12-month projections with 10x burst headroom.
steps.done_when: nfr_performance and nfr_scalability exist with measurable targets for all key metrics.

steps.name: nfr_availability_and_security
steps.instruction: Define availability NFRs: uptime_target, maintenance_windows, maximum_unplanned_downtime, RTO, RPO, degraded_mode_behavior per dependency. Then define security requirements covering: authentication, authorization, data_protection, api_security, infrastructure, audit, compliance. Map each regulatory_requirement to specific technical controls.
steps.done_when: nfr_availability and security_requirements exist with measurable targets and traceable security controls.

steps.name: data_requirements
steps.instruction: Define data requirements: for each entity: entity_name, description, data_fields, sensitivity_level, volume_estimate, retention_policy, deletion_requirements, backup_requirements. Also: data_flows, data_transformations, data_access_patterns, data_archival_strategy.
steps.done_when: data_requirements exists with all data entities, sensitivity, volume, retention, and access patterns defined.

steps.name: integration_requirements
steps.instruction:
Identify all third-party integrations needed. For each: integration_id, service_name, purpose, api_type, authentication_method, rate_limits, pricing, data_exchanged, latency_expectation, failure_mode, alternatives.

IMPORTANT: After using tools (max 2-3 calls), produce your answer as structured text or JSON in your final response. Your response IS the artifact.
steps.done_when: integration_requirements exists with every third-party service identified, evaluated, and failure modes documented.

steps.name: platform_and_accessibility_requirements
steps.instruction:
Define platform support: primary_platform, browser_support, mobile_support, screen_sizes, os_versions, device_types. If multiple platforms equally important, trigger needs_clarification. Define accessibility requirements: target WCAG 2.1 AA minimum. Organized by WCAG principle: perceivable, operable, understandable, robust. Each with req_id, WCAG criterion reference, implementation guidance, testing method. Define i18n requirements: initial_languages, future_languages, rtl_support, date/number/currency/timezone handling, text_expansion_budget, string_extraction_strategy.

IMPORTANT: After using tools (max 2-3 calls), produce your answer as structured text or JSON in your final response. Your response IS the artifact.
steps.done_when: platform_requirements, accessibility_requirements, and i18n_requirements exist.

steps.name: business_rules_extraction
steps.instruction: Extract every business rule: rule_id (BR-001...), title, rule_statement ('IF condition THEN consequence'), category, source, affected_features, testable_assertion.
steps.done_when: business_rules exists as a numbered list of precise, testable business rule statements.

steps.name: acceptance_criteria_audit_and_fixes
steps.instruction: Audit all user story acceptance criteria: has Given/When/Then, happy path covered, error/edge case covered, specific not vague, automatable, business rules reflected, no ambiguous terms. Flag any FR or BR with no corresponding acceptance criteria. For every failed story, rewrite/add acceptance criteria to address issues. Use Given/When/Then format. Ensure specific, testable, complete. Output complete updated user_stories_refined (all stories).
steps.done_when: user_stories_refined exists with all acceptance criteria audited and issues resolved.

steps.name: requirements_traceability
steps.instruction: Build traceability matrix: map every functional requirement to user stories and business rules. Identify any requirement without a user story or business rule.
steps.done_when: traceability_matrix exists with all requirements cross-referenced and orphans flagged.

steps.name: requirements_conflict_check
steps.instruction: Check for conflicts between functional requirements and NFRs. Identify where performance targets conflict with feature scope, where security constraints limit features, where scalability needs affect architecture. Document each conflict with resolution.
steps.done_when: conflict_resolutions exists with all conflicts identified and resolved.

steps.name: requirements_spec_functional
steps.instruction: Compile Part 1 of Requirements Specification: Functional Requirements, User Stories, Business Rules, Data Requirements. Format as structured document.
steps.done_when: requirements_spec_part1 exists as a structured document covering functional requirements.

steps.name: requirements_spec_nonfunctional
steps.instruction: Merge requirements_spec_part1 with non-functional requirements (performance, availability, security) and traceability matrix into the final Requirements Specification Document.
steps.done_when: requirements_spec exists as a comprehensive versioned document incorporating all requirement categories.

steps.name: requirements_review
steps.instruction: Review requirements_spec against prd_final for completeness, consistency, testability, and feasibility. Check that every user story has traceable requirements, no orphaned requirements exist, NFR targets are realistic, and security requirements cover all sensitive areas. Output status and issues list.
steps.done_when: requirements_review_result exists with comprehensive assessment.

steps.name: architecture_pattern_selection
steps.instruction:
Evaluate architecture patterns: monolith, modular_monolith, microservices, serverless, event_driven, hybrid. For each: pros/cons for our requirements, scale fit, team size fit, implementation/operational complexity, migration path. Recommend top 2. If top 2 have significantly different trade-offs, trigger needs_clarification. Output: selected_pattern, justification, constraints, migration_path.

IMPORTANT: After using tools (max 2-3 calls), produce your answer as structured text or JSON in your final response. Your response IS the artifact.
steps.done_when: architecture_pattern_decision exists with pattern evaluation and selection.

steps.name: tech_stack_research_and_selection
steps.instruction:
Research and select complete tech stack. For frontend: evaluate React/Next.js, Vue/Nuxt, Svelte/SvelteKit, Angular, Solid. For backend: evaluate Node.js (Express/Fastify/NestJS/Hono), Python (FastAPI/Django), Go, Rust. For database: evaluate PostgreSQL, MySQL, MongoDB, SQLite, PlanetScale, Supabase. For infra: evaluate AWS, GCP, Azure, Vercel, Railway, Render, Fly.io. Use recommend_stack tool if available. Weight AI-code-generation quality heavily. Include cost estimates. Trigger needs_clarification for human approval. Output complete tech_stack_decision.

IMPORTANT: After using tools (max 2-3 calls), produce your answer as structured text or JSON in your final response. Your response IS the artifact.
steps.done_when: tech_stack_decision exists with all stack choices, justifications, and cost estimates.

steps.name: system_architecture_design
steps.instruction:
Design high-level system architecture: components, databases, external_services, communication_patterns, data_flow, network_topology, environments.

OUTPUT SIZE: Keep system architecture under 2000 words. Focus on components and interactions.
steps.done_when: system_architecture exists with complete system design.

steps.name: database_schema_design
steps.instruction:
Design complete database schema: tables, columns, types, constraints, indexes, foreign_keys, enums. Normalized to 3NF minimum. Then self-review for: normalization, missing indexes, N+1 risks, missing constraints, scalability, naming consistency, audit columns, soft delete, business rule enforcement at DB level.

OUTPUT SIZE: Keep database schema under 1500 words. List tables, key columns, relationships.
steps.done_when: database_schema exists with comprehensive, self-reviewed schema design.

steps.name: api_design
steps.instruction:
Define API resource model: resources, base URLs, sub-resources, relationships, filterable/sortable/searchable fields, versioning strategy, pagination strategy, response envelope format, id format. Then design every API endpoint with: method, path, auth, roles, parameters, request/response schemas, error responses, rate limits, side effects. Convert into formal OpenAPI 3.1 specification with paths, schemas, security schemes, tags, and examples.

OUTPUT SIZE: Keep API design under 2000 words. List endpoints with method, path, description.
steps.done_when: openapi_spec exists as a complete OpenAPI 3.1 specification.

steps.name: auth_system_design
steps.instruction:
Design complete auth system combining authentication and authorization. Authentication: registration, oauth, login, session/JWT strategy, refresh mechanism, logout, password reset, password hashing, MFA if required. Authorization: RBAC roles/permissions, resource ownership, enforcement points, super admin, permission checking logic. Output a unified auth_design document.

IMPORTANT: After using tools (max 2-3 calls), produce your answer as structured text or JSON in your final response. Your response IS the artifact.
steps.done_when: auth_design exists with both authentication and authorization design.

steps.name: frontend_architecture_design
steps.instruction: Design frontend architecture: folder_structure, component_hierarchy, state_management, routing, data_fetching, code_splitting, error_boundaries, environment config.
steps.done_when: frontend_architecture exists with complete frontend design.

steps.name: third_party_service_selection
steps.instruction:
For each integration, recommend a vendor with pricing, setup complexity, SDK quality, lock-in risk. Calculate total monthly cost. Trigger needs_clarification if cost exceeds expectations.

IMPORTANT: After using tools (max 2-3 calls), produce your answer as structured text or JSON in your final response. Your response IS the artifact.
steps.done_when: third_party_selections exists with vendor recommendations and total cost.

steps.name: infrastructure_designs
steps.instruction: Design three infrastructure subsystems. Caching: technology, layers, per-endpoint decisions, patterns, invalidation, session cache, rate limit storage, cache warming, failure handling. Background jobs: technology, job types, queue config, retry strategy, dead letter handling, scheduled jobs, concurrency limits, monitoring. File storage: service, upload flow (presigned URLs), organization, validation, size limits, image processing, CDN delivery, cleanup, virus scanning.
steps.done_when: caching_strategy, background_job_design, and file_storage_design exist.

steps.name: communication_designs
steps.instruction: Design three communication subsystems. Email: service, email types, template system, sending architecture, delivery monitoring, preferences, dev mode, domain authentication. Realtime: if realtime features needed, design technology, connection management, channel model, event types, scaling, fallback. If not needed, document decision with future migration path. Search: technology choice matched to complexity level, indexed entities, sync strategy, search API design.
steps.done_when: email_system_design, realtime_design, and search_system_design exist.

steps.name: observability_strategy
steps.instruction: Design unified observability covering error handling, logging, and monitoring. Error handling: response format, error code taxonomy, HTTP status mapping, logging rules, client error handling, validation format, unhandled errors, circuit breakers. Logging: structured format, levels, per-request logging, correlation ID, PII scrubbing, retention, aggregation, alerting. Monitoring: health checks, RED/USE metrics, application metrics, infrastructure metrics, alert rules, severity levels, dashboards, on-call process.
steps.done_when: observability_strategy exists with error handling, logging, and monitoring designs.

steps.name: scalability_and_dr_plan
steps.instruction: Document per-component scaling: type, triggers, limits, database scaling, cache scaling, cost at scale. Define milestones: 100, 1K, 10K, 100K users. Then design DR: backup strategy, restore procedure, RTO/RPO targets, failure scenarios with response procedures, restore testing, multi-region strategy, incident communication.
steps.done_when: scalability_plan and disaster_recovery_plan exist.

steps.name: data_flow_diagrams
steps.instruction: Create detailed data flow descriptions for every major operation tracing data from browser through all components to database and back, including error branches.
steps.done_when: data_flow_diagrams exists with data flows for all major operations.

steps.name: architecture_decisions
steps.instruction: Write Architecture Decision Records (ADRs) for every major decision: architecture pattern, frontend/backend framework, database, hosting, auth approach, each vendor. Each ADR: title, status, context, decision, consequences.
steps.done_when: adrs exists with ADRs for all major decisions.

steps.name: backend_core_design
steps.instruction: Compile core backend architecture: System Architecture Overview, Database Schema & Data Model, API Design & Endpoints, Auth & Authorization. Summarize key decisions and patterns for each.
steps.done_when: backend_core_compilation exists covering core backend architecture sections.

steps.name: backend_services_design
steps.instruction: Merge backend_core_compilation with service designs: Caching Strategy, File Storage, Email System, Search System. Produce complete backend design compilation.
steps.done_when: backend_design_compilation exists as a comprehensive document covering all backend architecture sections.

steps.name: technical_design_document
steps.instruction: Merge backend_design_compilation with frontend architecture, infrastructure, and operational concerns into the final Technical Design Document. Add sections: Frontend Architecture, Observability & Monitoring, Scalability & Disaster Recovery, Data Flow Diagrams, Architecture Decision Records. Include table of contents and version number.
steps.done_when: technical_design_document exists as a comprehensive versioned document.

steps.name: architecture_review
steps.instruction: Review TDD for: every FR addressable, all NFRs have design elements, no SPOFs, security findings addressed, consistency, feasibility, over-engineering check. Also review auth design, file storage, and other security-sensitive areas specifically.
steps.done_when: architecture_review_result exists with comprehensive assessment.

steps.name: information_architecture
steps.instruction: Define app's information architecture: sitemap, content grouping, navigation model, page types.
steps.done_when: information_architecture exists.

steps.name: navigation_design
steps.instruction: Design navigation: primary, secondary, user menu, notifications, search, breadcrumbs, mobile, keyboard shortcuts, deep linking, back behavior.
steps.done_when: navigation_design exists.

steps.name: user_flows
steps.instruction: Diagram all user flows screen-by-screen. Auth flows: signup email, signup oauth, login email, login oauth, password reset, logout. Onboarding: account creation to first value moment, under 2 minutes, including abandonment handling and progressive disclosure. Core features: for each core feature, diagram complete user flow with all screens, transitions, success/error/loading/empty states, confirmations, undo support. Settings: profile, password change, email change, notifications, billing, payment, account deletion, connected accounts.
steps.done_when: All four user flow artifacts exist with screen-by-screen diagrams.

steps.name: layout_and_wireframes
steps.instruction: Define page layout system: app shell, layout templates, grid system, spacing scale, responsive breakpoints, per-breakpoint changes. Then create wireframe specifications for every unique screen in the MVP: content zones, element hierarchy, interactive elements, conditional elements.
steps.done_when: layout_system_spec and wireframe_specs exist.

steps.name: forms_and_states
steps.instruction: Based on wireframe_specs, define every form in the app: fields with validation rules, field grouping, multi-step config if applicable, submit/cancel behavior, auto-save, keyboard behavior. Then define all empty states (first-time, search no results, filtered no results, list empty, offline) and error states (404, 403, 500, maintenance, session expired, rate limited).
steps.done_when: form_specs and empty_error_state_specs exist.

steps.name: wireframe_review
steps.instruction: Review wireframes against user stories and flows: coverage, dead ends, pattern consistency, form validation, empty/error states, navigation reachability.
steps.done_when: wireframe_review_result exists.

steps.name: brand_and_design_tokens
steps.instruction: Define brand identity: personality, tone of voice, color psychology, typography personality, visual style direction. If multiple valid directions, trigger needs_clarification. Then define all design tokens as exact values: colors (primary 50-950, semantic, background, text, border), typography, spacing, borders, shadows, transitions, z-index. All in hex/px/rem.
steps.done_when: brand_identity and design_tokens exist with complete specifications.

steps.name: component_specs
steps.instruction: Specify every primitive component: Button, Input, Textarea, Select, Checkbox, Radio, Toggle, Label, Badge, Avatar, Icon, Divider, Spinner, Skeleton, Link, Tag. Each with variants, sizes, states, props, accessibility, responsive behavior. Then specify composite components: Card, Modal, Toast, Alert, Dropdown, Tooltip, Popover, Tabs, Accordion, Table, Pagination, Breadcrumb, EmptyState, Sidebar, Navbar, Footer. Each with composition from primitives, variants, sizes, states, props.
steps.done_when: component_spec_primitives and component_spec_composite exist with all component specifications.

steps.name: screen_specifications
steps.instruction: For every screen: component tree, data bindings, action handlers, conditional rendering, loading/error states, responsive overrides.
steps.done_when: screen_specifications exists.

steps.name: interaction_responsive_accessibility_specs
steps.instruction: Define all micro-interactions: transitions, feedback, loading behavior, scroll behavior, drag behavior. Include reduced-motion behavior. For each screen at every breakpoint: layout changes, hidden elements, navigation changes, component swaps, touch adaptations, font adjustments, image handling. Per-screen accessibility: focus order, ARIA labels, live regions, keyboard shortcuts, skip links, landmarks, heading hierarchy, form accessibility, color independence.
steps.done_when: interaction_spec, responsive_spec, and accessibility_spec exist.

steps.name: design_review
steps.instruction: Review design specs for coverage, consistency, component reuse, accessibility completeness, responsive completeness, implementability, token compliance. Evaluate design token choices for color harmony, typography hierarchy, spacing consistency, competitive positioning, visual coherence.
steps.done_when: design_review_result exists.

steps.name: design_system_handoff
steps.instruction: Compile design system for handoff: Design Tokens (colors, typography, spacing), Primitive Components (Button, Input, etc.), Composite Components (Card, Modal, etc.), and review notes.
steps.done_when: design_system_compilation exists covering tokens, primitives, and composites.

steps.name: design_handoff_document
steps.instruction: Merge design_system_compilation with screen specs, interaction patterns, responsive rules, and accessibility requirements into the final Design Handoff Document.
steps.done_when: design_handoff exists as a comprehensive document.

steps.name: epic_definition_and_work_breakdown
steps.instruction: Group user stories into epics with: epic_id, name, description, user_stories, dependencies, estimated_total_effort, priority_order. Order by dependency, value, risk. Then break every user story into implementation tasks following the feature template pattern: db_migration, backend, frontend, tests, e2e, code_review. Each with task_id, story_id, epic_id, agent, dependencies, estimated_effort.
steps.done_when: epics and work_breakdown exist with all stories grouped and decomposed.

steps.name: dependency_graph_and_estimation
steps.instruction: Build complete task dependency graph. Identify: critical_path, parallelizable_groups, bottleneck_tasks. Then refine effort estimates (XS/S/M/L/XL) with confidence levels. Flag XL tasks for decomposition. Calculate totals and identify risky estimates.
steps.done_when: dependency_graph and effort_estimates exist.

steps.name: risk_assessment
steps.instruction: Identify risks across: technical, integration, scope, dependency, security, operational. Each with: risk_id, description, category, likelihood, impact, risk_score, early_warning_signs, mitigation_strategy, contingency_plan.
steps.done_when: risk_assessment exists with all risks scored, with mitigation and contingency plans.

steps.name: sprint_planning
steps.instruction: Plan Sprint 0: repository setup, CI/CD, Docker, backend/frontend scaffolding, design system, database setup, test framework, staging provisioning, developer docs. Then plan all feature sprints: each delivering a demonstrable increment, balanced frontend/backend, 80% capacity for buffer, including review and testing tasks.
steps.done_when: sprint_0_plan and sprint_plans exist.

steps.name: milestones_and_dod
steps.instruction: Define milestones: alpha, beta, release_candidate, general_availability. Each with target_sprint, entry/exit criteria, risks_to_timeline. Define DoD at: task (code, lint, type check, tests, self-review), story (all tasks, acceptance criteria, code review, no critical bugs, deployed to staging), sprint (all stories, regression suite, no critical bugs, stable staging), release (all sprints, security audit, perf targets, docs, legal, infra).
steps.done_when: milestones and definition_of_done exist.

steps.name: project_plan_review
steps.instruction: Review project plan: all stories assigned, dependencies respected, balanced loads, realistic milestones, risk mitigations as tasks, testing in every sprint, buffer exists. If timeline unrealistic, trigger needs_clarification.
steps.done_when: project_plan_review_result exists. Timeline is realistic or human has approved adjusted scope.

steps.name: repository_setup
steps.instruction: Initialize the repository using the scaffold tool if applicable. Create the repository structure, .gitignore for the detected tech stack, .editorconfig, README stub. Initialize git, set up branching strategy (main + develop), make initial commit. Configure git hooks directory.
steps.done_when: repository_created and git_config_files exist with initialized repo and git configuration.

steps.name: linting_and_formatting
steps.instruction: Set up code formatting (Prettier/Black/rustfmt), linting (ESLint/Pylint/clippy), and pre-commit hooks (husky/pre-commit). Configure all tools with project-appropriate rules. Set up editor configuration files (.vscode/settings.json, .editorconfig). Ensure pre-commit hooks run formatter and linter.
steps.done_when: linter_config, formatter_config, and precommit_config exist and pre-commit hooks run correctly.

steps.name: backend_scaffold
steps.instruction: Use the scaffold tool to initialize the backend project with the selected framework. Set up: project structure matching system_architecture, TypeScript/language configuration (strict mode), environment management (.env.example with all required vars), base middleware (CORS, body parsing, request logging per observability_strategy, error handling per observability_strategy), API validation library, health check endpoint. Install and verify all dependencies.
steps.done_when: backend_scaffold exists with a running backend that responds to health checks.

steps.name: backend_scaffold_git_commit
steps.instruction: Mechanical auto-commit after 7.3.
steps.done_when: Workspace committed if there are changes.

steps.name: database_setup
steps.instruction: Set up database client/ORM with connection pooling and configuration. Set up migration system with initial migration matching database_schema. Create seed data script with development data based on user_personas and business_rules. Verify migrations run forward and backward. Verify seed script populates data correctly.
steps.done_when: db_client, migration_system, and seed_script exist and work correctly.

steps.name: frontend_scaffold
steps.instruction: Use the scaffold tool to initialize the frontend project with the selected framework. Set up: project structure matching frontend_architecture, TypeScript configuration (strict mode), routing configuration per information_architecture, state management per frontend_architecture, API client with auth token handling and error handling per observability_strategy. Install and verify all dependencies.
steps.done_when: frontend_scaffold exists with a running frontend dev server.

steps.name: frontend_scaffold_git_commit
steps.instruction: Mechanical auto-commit after 7.5.
steps.done_when: Workspace committed if there are changes.

steps.name: test_infrastructure
steps.instruction: Set up test infrastructure for both backend and frontend. Backend: test framework (Jest/Vitest/pytest), test database configuration, test utilities (factories, fixtures, mocking), coverage reporting. Frontend: component testing (React Testing Library/Vue Test Utils), test utilities, MSW or equivalent for API mocking, coverage reporting. Write at least one example test for each to verify the setup works.
steps.done_when: backend_test_config and frontend_test_config exist with passing example tests.

steps.name: docker_setup
steps.instruction: Create Docker setup: backend Dockerfile (multi-stage build), frontend Dockerfile (multi-stage build), docker-compose.yml with all services (backend, frontend, database, cache if needed). Configure volumes, networking, environment variables, and health checks. Verify the full stack starts and runs with docker-compose up.
steps.done_when: docker_compose_config exists and docker-compose up works.

steps.name: ci_cd_pipeline
steps.instruction: Set up CI pipeline (GitHub Actions/GitLab CI/etc.): lint, type check, unit tests, integration tests, build, security scan. Set up CD pipeline for staging (auto-deploy on merge to develop) and production (deploy on tag/release). Configure caching for dependencies. Configure test result and coverage reporting.
steps.done_when: ci_config, cd_staging_config, and cd_production_config exist.

steps.name: design_system_implementation
steps.instruction: Implement design tokens as CSS variables/theme configuration and the base layout system. Convert all design tokens to code (CSS custom properties, Tailwind theme, or styled-system). Implement the app shell layout, responsive grid system, and base navigation components. Verify all tokens render correctly at all breakpoints.
steps.done_when: design_tokens_code and layout_components exist with verified rendering.

steps.name: primitive_components
steps.instruction: Implement all primitive components from component_spec_primitives: Button, Input, Textarea, Select, Checkbox, Radio, Toggle, Label, Badge, Avatar, Icon, Divider, Spinner, Skeleton, Link, Tag. Each with all variants, sizes, states, and accessibility attributes. Use design tokens for all styling.
steps.done_when: primitive_components_code exists with all primitives implemented.

steps.name: composite_components
steps.instruction: Implement all composite components from component_spec_composite: Card, Modal, Toast, Alert, Dropdown, Tooltip, Popover, Tabs, Accordion, Table, Pagination, Breadcrumb, EmptyState, Sidebar, Navbar, Footer. Compose from primitive components. Include animations per interaction_spec.
steps.done_when: composite_components_code exists with all composites implemented.

steps.name: component_playground
steps.instruction: Set up a component playground (Storybook, Ladle, or equivalent) with stories for every primitive and composite component. Each story should show all variants, sizes, and states.
steps.done_when: storybook_setup exists with stories for all components.

steps.name: staging_environment
steps.instruction: Provision staging environment and deploy the scaffolded application. Verify health checks pass, database migrations run, and the application is accessible. Test the CD pipeline by triggering a deployment.
steps.done_when: staging_environment and staging_deployment_verified exist.

steps.name: sprint_0_verification
steps.instruction: Run comprehensive smoke tests on the staging environment: verify backend health endpoint, verify frontend loads, verify database connection, verify docker-compose works locally, verify CI pipeline passes, verify linting/formatting runs.
steps.done_when: sprint_0_smoke_result exists with all checks passing.

steps.name: developer_workflow_docs
steps.instruction: Write developer workflow documentation covering: how to set up the local development environment, how to run the application, how to run tests, how to create a new feature branch, how to deploy to staging, coding conventions to follow.
steps.done_when: developer_workflow_doc exists.

steps.name: sprint_0_review
steps.instruction: Review all Sprint 0 deliverables: repository structure, CI/CD pipelines, Docker setup, design system components, test infrastructure, staging environment, developer documentation. Verify everything works end-to-end. Run index_workspace to build the codebase index â€” this enables query_codebase for all subsequent implementation steps.
steps.done_when: sprint_0_review_result exists.

steps.name: implementation_context_package
steps.instruction: Create a condensed implementation reference. For each major subsystem: the 3-5 most important design decisions, the specific patterns to follow, the key constraints. This document replaces the need for implementer agents to read the full TDD. Target: under 5000 words covering everything an implementer needs for day-to-day coding decisions.
steps.done_when: implementation_context exists as a concise (<5000 words) reference for all implementation work.

steps.name: implementation_backlog_initialization
steps.instruction: Initialize the executable implementation backlog. Transform sprint_plans into a flat, ordered list of features to implement. Each entry: feature_id, feature_name, epic_id, sprint_id, user_story_ids, depends_on_features, status (pending/in_progress/completed/blocked), template_ref ('feature_implementation_template'). Order respecting dependencies and sprint assignments.
steps.done_when: implementation_backlog exists as an ordered list of features, each referencing the implementation template.

steps.name: feasibility_spike
steps.instruction: Execute feasibility spikes for the highest-risk items identified in risk_assessment. For each spike: build a minimal proof-of-concept testing the risky integration or architecture decision. Verify: third-party APIs work as documented, performance-critical paths meet NFR targets at prototype scale, complex algorithms produce correct results. If any spike fails, trigger needs_clarification with alternatives. Document findings and any design adjustments needed.
steps.done_when: spike_results exists with proof-of-concept results for all high-risk items.

steps.name: feasibility_spike_git_commit
steps.instruction: Mechanical auto-commit after 8.spike.
steps.done_when: Workspace committed if there are changes.

steps.name: sprint_boundary_check
steps.instruction: Sprint boundary checkpoint. Execute when all features assigned to current sprint are completed. Summarize what was built, run regression suite, compare velocity, identify slips, review next sprint. If velocity >30% below plan, trigger needs_clarification. Output: sprint_id_completed, features_completed, velocity_ratio, next_sprint_adjustments.
steps.done_when: Sprint is reviewed, next sprint is confirmed or adjusted.
steps.trigger: Execute after the last feature of each sprint completes

steps.name: architecture_alignment_check
steps.instruction: Compare what was actually built this sprint against the technical_design_document. Identify deviations classified as: intentional_improvement (keep code, update design doc), accidental_drift (fix code to match design), design_was_wrong (design needs to change to match reality). For 'design_was_wrong' items: write a mini-ADR documenting what changed and why, update the relevant section of technical_design_document in-place.
steps.done_when: architecture_deviation_report exists and any design doc updates have been applied.
steps.trigger: Execute at every sprint boundary alongside sprint_boundary_check

steps.name: coverage_analysis
steps.instruction: Run the project's test suite with coverage reporting enabled. Analyze the coverage output to identify which modules, functions, and branches are untested. Produce a coverage_report listing: overall line/branch/function coverage percentages, per-module coverage breakdown, and a prioritized list of uncovered critical paths (auth, payment, data mutations).
steps.done_when: coverage_report exists.

steps.name: test_gap_filling
steps.instruction: Fill testing gaps identified in coverage_report. Write critical path tests for login, signup, checkout, data CRUD, permission checks â€” happy path and primary failure mode. Write negative scenario tests: invalid inputs, unauthorized access, expired tokens, malformed requests, missing required fields, rate limits. Write boundary value tests for all numeric inputs, string length limits, date ranges, pagination parameters, file size limits â€” test zero, one, max, max+1, negative, empty. Follow project test patterns.
steps.done_when: critical_path_tests, negative_tests, and boundary_tests exist.

steps.name: concurrency_and_integrity_tests
steps.instruction: Write concurrency tests: simultaneous writes to the same resource, concurrent user sessions, parallel API requests to stateful endpoints, database transaction conflicts. Write data integrity tests: foreign key constraints, cascading deletes, unique constraints, required fields, data type validations at DB level, transaction rollback.
steps.done_when: concurrency_tests and integrity_tests exist.

steps.name: e2e_test_suite
steps.instruction: Review existing E2E tests against functional requirements. Write missing E2E tests for user flows without coverage. Each test should simulate a complete user flow from entry to completion, including UI interactions, API calls, and database state verification. Use the project's E2E framework (Playwright, Cypress, or equivalent).
steps.done_when: e2e_test_suite exists with comprehensive E2E coverage.

steps.name: performance_testing
steps.instruction: Create performance test scripts using k6, Artillery, or equivalent. Write scripts for: API endpoint response time benchmarks, database query performance under load, concurrent user simulation (10, 50, 100, 500 users), throughput tests for highest-traffic endpoints. Then run them against staging to establish baseline metrics. Capture p50/p95/p99 response times, throughput, error rates, CPU/memory utilization.
steps.done_when: performance_test_scripts and baseline_performance_results exist.

steps.name: stress_test_and_optimization
steps.instruction: Run stress tests at 2x, 5x, 10x expected load. Identify breaking point and bottlenecks. Then implement optimizations: database indexes, query caching, N+1 fixes, connection pooling, compression, pagination, lazy-loading. Re-run performance tests to verify improvements. Document before/after metrics.
steps.done_when: stress_test_results and performance_optimizations exist with before/after comparison.

steps.name: cross_platform_testing
steps.instruction: Run tests and key user flows in target browsers (Chrome, Firefox, Safari, Edge) and on mobile devices/screen sizes (320px, 375px, 414px, 768px). Verify layout, JS functionality, forms, animations, touch interactions, virtual keyboard behavior. Document any failures with screenshots.
steps.done_when: cross_browser_results and mobile_test_results exist.

steps.name: cross_platform_fixes
steps.instruction: Fix all critical and major cross-platform issues. Apply CSS fixes, vendor prefixes, flexbox/grid fallbacks, responsive breakpoint adjustments, touch target sizing. Verify each fix in the affected browser/device.
steps.done_when: cross_platform_fixes exists with all critical/major issues resolved.

steps.name: accessibility_testing_and_fixes
steps.instruction: Run automated accessibility scanning (axe-core, Pa11y, or Lighthouse) against WCAG 2.1 AA. Test keyboard navigation for all interactive elements. Check color contrast for all text/background combinations. Fix all critical and serious violations: missing form labels, keyboard traps, zero-contrast text, missing alt text, broken ARIA. Re-run scans after fixes to verify.
steps.done_when: a11y_scan_results and a11y_fixes_applied exist with all critical/serious issues resolved.

steps.name: full_regression_suite
steps.instruction: Run the complete test suite including unit tests, integration tests, E2E tests, performance tests, and accessibility tests as a full regression after all Phase 9 fixes. All tests must pass. If any fail, investigate whether they are pre-existing flaky tests or regressions from the fixes.
steps.done_when: regression_results exists with all tests passing.

steps.name: bug_compilation
steps.instruction: Compile all bugs discovered during Phase 9 testing into a structured bug_report. For each bug: assign a unique ID, severity (P0-P3), category, reproduction steps, expected vs actual behavior, affected browsers/devices, and suggested fix approach. Sort by severity.
steps.done_when: bug_report exists.

steps.name: owasp_audit
steps.instruction: Conduct a systematic OWASP Top 10 security audit. Review the codebase and running application against each category: Injection, Broken Authentication, Sensitive Data Exposure, XXE, Broken Access Control, Security Misconfiguration, XSS, Insecure Deserialization, Vulnerable Components, Insufficient Logging. For each category, document: risk level, evidence found, and remediation steps.
steps.done_when: owasp_audit_result exists.

steps.name: injection_and_xss_testing
steps.instruction: Test all user input points for SQL injection, NoSQL injection, LDAP injection, OS command injection, and ORM injection. Test API parameters, query strings, form fields, HTTP headers, file upload filenames. Also test all output points for reflected XSS, stored XSS, and DOM-based XSS. Verify output encoding and Content-Security-Policy headers.
steps.done_when: injection_test_result and xss_test_result exist.

steps.name: auth_and_authz_testing
steps.instruction: Review authentication: password hashing (bcrypt/scrypt/argon2), session tokens (random, long), token expiration/refresh, account lockout, password reset flow. Test authorization: horizontal privilege escalation (user A accessing user B's data), vertical escalation (regular user accessing admin), IDOR vulnerabilities, role-based access at API and data layers. Test CSRF: tokens on all forms, SameSite cookie, custom headers for AJAX.
steps.done_when: auth_security_result, authz_test_result, and csrf_test_result exist.

steps.name: sast_and_dependency_scan
steps.instruction: Run dependency vulnerability scanning (npm audit, pip audit, Snyk, Trivy). Scan codebase and git history for exposed secrets (gitleaks, truffleHog, detect-secrets). Audit HTTP security headers (CSP, HSTS, X-Content-Type-Options, X-Frame-Options, Referrer-Policy, Permissions-Policy). Review API security: auth on non-public endpoints, request validation, no internal detail leakage, rate limiting, request size limits.
steps.done_when: dependency_scan_result, secrets_audit_result, and security_headers_audit_result exist.

steps.name: encryption_and_logging_review
steps.instruction: Verify encryption: all data in transit uses TLS 1.2+, sensitive data at rest is encrypted, database connections use SSL, encryption keys stored securely, key rotation exists. Review logging security: passwords and tokens never logged, PII masked, log injection prevented, log files have appropriate permissions, error logs don't expose internals.
steps.done_when: encryption_verification_result and logging_security_result exist.

steps.name: dependency_and_header_fixes
steps.instruction: Fix all critical and high severity dependency vulnerabilities: upgrade to patched versions, assess breaking changes for major upgrades, document accepted risks. Implement all missing or misconfigured security headers: CSP with nonce-based script allowlisting, HSTS with 1-year max-age, all recommended headers. Run test suite after each change.
steps.done_when: dependency_fixes and security_headers_implemented exist.

steps.name: file_upload_security
steps.instruction: Review file upload implementation: server-side type validation, malware scanning, size limits, filename sanitization (path traversal prevention), files stored outside web root, content-disposition headers force download, image re-encoding to strip EXIF/metadata.
steps.done_when: file_upload_security_result exists.

steps.name: security_triage
steps.instruction: Compile all security test results into a prioritized triage list. For each finding: severity (critical/high/medium/low), description, affected component, suggested fix. Sort by severity. Output as structured JSON.
steps.done_when: security_triage exists with all findings prioritized by severity.

steps.name: security_fixes
steps.instruction: Apply fixes for all critical and high severity findings from security_triage. For each fix: describe what was changed, verify the fix doesn't break functionality. Also address any issues from encryption, logging, file upload, and secrets audits.
steps.done_when: security_fixes_applied exists with all critical and high fixes applied.

steps.name: security_retest_and_report
steps.instruction: Re-run all security tests to verify fixes resolved vulnerabilities without introducing new ones. Then write a comprehensive security audit report: executive summary with overall security posture rating, methodology, vulnerabilities found by category and severity, remediation actions taken, current status, remaining risks, and recommendations.
steps.done_when: security_retest_results and security_audit_report exist.

steps.name: api_and_developer_docs
steps.instruction: Generate comprehensive API documentation from openapi_spec. Write a developer setup guide covering: prerequisites, cloning, env vars, dependency installation, database setup, running dev server, running tests, common tasks. Write an architecture overview covering: system diagram, tech stack rationale, data flow, directory structure, key patterns, external integrations.
steps.done_when: api_docs, developer_setup_guide, and architecture_overview exist.

steps.name: user_documentation
steps.instruction: Write a getting-started guide for new users covering signup, initial config, first-time walkthrough, key concepts. Write detailed feature documentation for every MVP feature.
